title: rumex aquaticus subsp. tomentellus
intro: Rumex aquaticus subsp. tomentellus, also known as woolly dock, is a subspecies of the Rumex aquaticus plant, a type of water dock that grows in aquatic environments. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of woolly dock include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Woolly dock can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, and it is also used in traditional cuisine in some parts of the world.
